lenses.guidance: vv 7-8 ground election not in Israel's size but in covenant love: 'because the LORD loved you and kept the oath he swore to your ancestors.' The redemption arc rests on God's prior promise, not human merit.

vv 7-8's election language is later echoed at Mal 1:2-3 ('Jacob I loved'), Rom 9:11-13 (which quotes that line), and Eph 1:4-5. The canonical thread is unbroken: chosen because loved, not loved because chosen.

vv 17-26's fear-not refrain — 'do not be afraid... the LORD your God is with you' — addresses the heart that counts numbers. To trust today is to remember what God did to Pharaoh (v 18) when the fear comes.
